attribution: GTFS Scorecard (gtfsscorecard.org), scored on top of the MobilityData gtfs-validator

problems.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
problems.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
problems.what: 146 of 163 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
problems.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

problems.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
problems.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
problems.what: 71 of 71 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
problems.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.

problems.effort: A quick look at the flagged files.
problems.fix: Check the flagged column names for misspellings of standard GTFS fields; remove them if they are vendor extras.
problems.what: Some files contain columns that are not part of the GTFS spec.
problems.why: Harmless to riders, but apps ignore these columns and they can hide typos in real column names.

problems.effort: Varies.
problems.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unknown_file' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
problems.what: Unknown file (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
problems.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

problems.effort: One setting in most export tools.
problems.fix: Remove past service periods the next time you export the feed.
problems.what: Some service calendars in the feed have already expired.
problems.why: Expired calendars are dead weight and can hide real schedule problems from your staff and vendors.

problems.effort: Varies.
problems.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'service_window_outside_feed_period' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
problems.what: Service window outside feed period (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
problems.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

problems.effort: Usually a bulk edit in your scheduling software.
problems.fix: Populate trip_headsign to match what the bus displays.
problems.what: 63 of 71 trips have no headsign.
problems.why: Riders at the stop can't tell which direction a bus is going.

problems.effort: A review pass in your scheduling software.
problems.fix: Remove retired stops from the export, or add them back to the trips that should serve them.
problems.what: Some stops exist in the feed but no trip ever stops at them.
problems.why: Riders may walk to a stop where no bus is scheduled to arrive.

problems.effort: One setting in most export tools.
problems.fix: Trim past service periods the next time you export.
problems.what: Many of the feed's trips don't run at all in the next 7 days.
problems.why: It usually means old service periods are still in the export, making the feed bigger and harder to check.

problems.effort: Usually one export setting; export on a schedule so it never lapses again.
problems.fix: Re-export the feed with a calendar that reaches further out, and set feed_info feed_end_date past your next service change.
problems.what: Service data ended 2741 day(s) ago.
problems.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency even though the buses are still running. Riders are told the service does not exist.

problems.effort: A small file for most flat-fare systems.
problems.fix: Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
problems.what: The feed contains no fare information.
problems.why: Riders see 'fare unknown' in trip planners and can't budget their trip; visitors are most affected.

problems.effort: A small file; one rule often covers the whole service.
problems.fix: Add booking_rules.txt saying how far ahead and how to book (phone, app, or web).
problems.what: This feed describes flexible (demand-responsive) service but has no booking_rules.txt.
problems.why: Riders can see the service area but not how or when to reserve a trip, so they can't actually use the service.

problems.effort: Usually a re-export from your scheduling software.
problems.fix: Export and publish an updated GTFS feed that covers at least the next 30 days of service.
problems.what: This feed's service calendar runs out within the next 7 days.
problems.why: When the calendar ends, Google Maps and other trip planners drop your agency entirely. Riders see no service at all.

problems.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
problems.fix: Use mixed case for stop names and headsigns (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave', not 'MAIN ST & 2ND AVE').
problems.what: Some rider-facing names are in ALL CAPS or all lowercase.
problems.why: ALL-CAPS stop and headsign names are harder to read in apps and are read awkwardly by screen readers.

problems.effort: One field.
problems.fix: Add feed_contact_email to feed_info.txt.
problems.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
problems.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

problems.effort: Varies.
problems.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'missing_feed_contact_email_and_url' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
problems.what: Missing feed contact email and url (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
problems.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

problems.effort: A field at a time; not urgent.
problems.fix: Review the flagged fields and fill in the ones your riders would use.
problems.what: Some files are missing recommended (not required) fields.
problems.why: Recommended fields like agency_phone or stop descriptions make the feed more useful to riders and trip planners.

problems.effort: A few minutes per flagged trip.
problems.fix: Check the flagged stop times for transposed minutes.
problems.what: Some scheduled trips travel implausibly fast between stops.
problems.why: Usually a typo'd stop time; riders get impossible arrival estimates.

problems.effort: Varies.
problems.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'missing_required_column' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
problems.what: Missing required column (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
problems.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

problems.effort: Two fields, set once in export settings.
problems.fix: Add feed_info.txt with feed_start_date and feed_end_date to your export.
problems.what: feed_info.txt is missing its start/end dates (the file itself is absent)
problems.why: Apps and this scorecard can't warn anyone before the feed goes stale without stated validity dates.

problems.effort: Varies.
problems.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_too_far_from_shape_using_user_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
problems.what: Stop too far from shape using user distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
problems.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

problems.effort: Varies.
problems.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'big_gap_in_service' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
problems.what: Big gap in service (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
problems.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

problems.effort: One small file, set once in export settings.
problems.fix: Add feed_info.txt with publisher name, URL, language, and feed_start_date/feed_end_date.
problems.what: A recommended GTFS file (usually feed_info.txt) is missing.
problems.why: feed_info.txt tells apps who publishes the feed and when it expires; without it nobody is warned before data goes stale.

problems.effort: Worth it for multi-level or large stations; flat stops don't need it.
problems.fix: Add pathways.txt connecting entrances, platforms, and any elevators, with a level for each.
problems.what: This feed models stations or entrances but has no pathways.txt.
problems.why: Trip planners can't guide riders through the station, and there is no step-free route information for wheelchair users.

problems.effort: A few minutes per flagged stop.
problems.fix: Check the flagged stops' coordinates and the route shape in your scheduling software; re-snap whichever is misplaced.
problems.what: Some stops sit far from the route line they belong to.
problems.why: Trip planners may draw the bus route through the wrong streets or point riders to the wrong corner.
